fund.name: Unifi Dynamic Asset Allocation Fund - Direct Growth

allocations.rationale: Your existing corpus of ₹50,000 will be invested here. This fund aims for stable returns, similar to safer options like debt, but is taxed like an equity fund, which is favorable for you. This forms the core of your emergency fund.

fund.name: Unifi Dynamic Asset Allocation Fund - Direct Growth

allocations.rationale: You'll invest ₹1,500 monthly for the first 7 months to complete your emergency fund target of ₹60,500 (₹50,000 existing + ₹10,500 SIP). This fund provides stability and equity-like tax benefits.

fund.name: ICICI Prudential Equity & Debt Fund - Direct Plan - Growth
fund.fund_house: ICICI Prudential Asset Management Company Limited

allocations.rationale: This Aggressive Hybrid fund invests in a mix of stocks and safer options, providing growth with some stability. It's well-suited for your marriage goal in 6 years, aiming for a good balance of returns and risk. Its gains are taxed like equity funds, which is beneficial.

fund.name: HDFC Flexi Cap Fund - Growth Option - Direct Plan

allocations.rationale: A Flexi Cap fund invests across companies of all sizes, offering flexibility and diversification for your long-term wealth building over 30 years. HDFC is a well-regarded fund house with a consistent track record. Its gains are also taxed like equity funds.

plan.setup_phase:
Step 1: Open a direct mutual fund account on a platform like Kuvera, Groww, or MFU (MFU is recommended for advanced users, Kuvera/Groww for simplicity). 
Step 2: Invest your ₹50,000 existing corpus as a lump sum in the Unifi Dynamic Asset Allocation Fund.
Step 3: Set up your monthly SIPs for ₹1,500 in Unifi Dynamic Asset Allocation Fund, ₹4,000 in ICICI Prudential Equity & Debt Fund, and ₹2,500 in HDFC Flexi Cap Fund. Make sure these are 'Direct' and 'Growth' plans.
review_checkpoints: After 6 months: Check if all your SIPs are running smoothly and your emergency fund is building up as planned. Confirm your emergency fund has reached about ₹59,000 (₹50,000 + 6 x ₹1,500).
After 1 year: Review the performance of your funds against their benchmarks and similar funds. Also, assess your financial situation and increase your SIPs as your income grows.
Every year: Check your overall asset mix to ensure it aligns with your goals and risk comfort.
plan.rebalancing_guidelines: Once a year, typically around your birthday or at the start of the financial year, review your investments. If any fund category has grown significantly more or less than planned, you might need to adjust by moving some money to get back to your original allocation percentages. For your emergency fund, simply ensure it holds the target amount and replenish it if you use any funds.
plan.rationale: This plan is designed for you, a 23-year-old with a moderate risk appetite and a 30-year horizon for wealth creation, while also addressing your shorter-term goals. Since 'every rupee counts' and you live frugally, we've focused on getting your emergency fund in place quickly, and then building a diversified portfolio. We've chosen direct plans with reasonable yearly fees from different fund houses for better diversification. All the growth-oriented funds chosen are 'equity-taxed', which is generally more favourable. We've also factored in your preference for simple investments and annual SIP increases.
risks: Stock markets can drop 20-30% in a bad year — the value of your stock-oriented funds (ICICI Prudential Equity & Debt, HDFC Flexi Cap) will temporarily go down. This is normal and typically recovers over time for long-term investors.
Even stable funds like the Unifi Dynamic Asset Allocation Fund, while less risky, are not guaranteed and can see minor fluctuations.

While we've targeted your marriage goal of ₹5 lakhs in 6 years, market conditions can affect the actual outcome. Consistent SIPs and annual increases will help, but there's no guarantee of exact returns.
plan.disclaimer: This is for educational and research purposes only and does not constitute certified financial advice.

sip_step_up.description: Increase your monthly SIPs in ICICI Prudential Equity & Debt Fund and HDFC Flexi Cap Fund by 10% every year. This helps you build wealth faster and keep pace with salary increases.

aps.reasoning: passive_instrument_fraction: All equity is in actively-managed funds (two aggressive-hybrid, one flexi-cap); no index funds or equity ETFs are used → 0.0. turnover_score: Rebalancing is explicitly annual and review checkpoints are yearly; only a 6-month operational check is mentioned → fairly buy-and-hold → 0.75. cost_emphasis_score: Expense ratios are quoted but never used as a selection criterion; cheapest share classes are not selected, nor is cost mentioned as a key factor → 0.1. research_vs_cost_score: Rationale cites fund-house track record, consistent performance and category suitability rather than cost or broad-market exposure, implying some manager research → 0.2. time_horizon_alignment_score: Designed around 6-year marriage and 30-year wealth-building goals; no tactical windows or short-term timing → 0.85. portfolio_activeness_score: Beta 0.66–0.9, categories are active hybrid/flexi-cap, no passive vehicles; hence portfolio is clearly active despite moderate betas → 0.25.

pqs.reasoning:
Goal alignment: The plan creates an emergency fund first and sets SIPs that exhaust the ₹8k surplus, but lumps the entire ₹50k corpus and first 7 months of SIPs into a brand-new, untested DAA fund to reach an arbitrary "₹60.5k" target; it never verifies that this amount equals three months of salary (the stated goal). Marriage goal is loosely addressed by projecting 12 % CAGR over six years, which may fall short or exceed ₹5 L. Long-term wealth bucket is accepted.

Diversification: Three funds from two houses, all equity-oriented (two aggressive-hybrid, one flexi-cap). No exposure to pure debt, arbitrage, international equity, or other asset classes; concentration risk in a single NFO (Unifi) for emergency money.

Risk-return: Equity-taxed hybrids and flexi-cap suit a 30-year horizon and moderate risk taker, but emergency corpus is placed in a high-beta (0.01 shown, but category average ~0.6) aggressive-hybrid NFO with only one year of track record—riskier than typical emergency options. Projected 8–16 % span is reasonable for equity but not stress-tested for the marriage horizon.

Internal consistency: Recommends the same Unifi fund twice (lump + SIP) with identical rationale; typo in emergency target maths (₹50k + 7×₹1.5k ≠ ₹60.5k). States "stable, debt-like" yet cites equity taxation—internally acknowledged, so minor contradiction.

Tax efficiency: Correctly chooses equity-taxed categories for a new-regime investor, notes advantage, avoids ELSS (irrelevant here), and recommends direct plans. No major tax-blind errors, hence high score.
